page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.medium is an ARM64-based compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating at 2.8 GHz, this single-core, single-threaded server features 2.0 GB of system memory. The processor architecture includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 59 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results show strong single-threaded performance in specific tasks, such as bzip3 decompression at 14.06 GB/sec. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is average at 1692.09, while gzip compression also registers in the average tier at 30.06 GB/sec. However, due to its single-core configuration, multi-core benchmarks and overall PassMark CPU scores are poor. Memory bandwidth is limited, with large-block write speeds at 11.92 GB/sec and read speeds at 30.12 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance are weak to poor, reflecting the resource constraints of a single-core, 2 GB memory profile.

The c8gd.medium offers a cost-efficient entry point into the Graviton8 family, combining dedicated ARMv9 compute resources with high-speed local NVMe SSD storage. The primary tradeoff is the low resource density, particularly the 2.0 GB memory limit and single-thread execution, which restricts its utility for memory-intensive or highly parallelized applications. It provides a balanced option for lightweight, disk-dependent workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of larger instance sizes.

This instance is suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic web serving, development environments, and small-scale database or caching tasks that require fast local scratch space but do not demand high memory capacity or multi-core processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.medium is a single-core, compute-optimized instance built on the ARM64 architecture using an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating at 2.8 GHz, it features 1 dedicated physical core, 2.0 GB of memory, and 59 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression and average single-core stress-ng performance, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are low due to its single-vCPU configuration. This instance represents a cost-efficient option for lightweight, disk-reliant workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, and single-threaded processing tasks that benefit from dedicated CPU resources and fast local storage without requiring extensive memory.

tagline: A single-core ARM64 compute instance featuring dedicated Graviton processing and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight workloads.

59 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
2.0 GB of system memory and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for lightweight web serving, development, and single-threaded tasks
